## Summary

<!-- One or two sentences: what does this PR do and why. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix or doc accuracy correction (non-breaking)
- [ ] New worked example (Layer 1 / 2 / 4)
- [ ] Improved scaffold prompt
- [ ] Migration guide improvement
- [ ] CI / tooling improvement
- [ ] README restructure
- [ ] Other (please describe)

## Scope check

The scaffold deliberately ships **structure and prompts**, not philosophy. Confirm your change respects this:

- [ ] My change does NOT add language or framework opinions
- [ ] My change does NOT add a curated rule library (one or two worked examples is the limit)
- [ ] My change does NOT add Claude Code feature documentation that belongs upstream
- [ ] My change does NOT impose a process/methodology on adopters
- [ ] If my change adds content, it's clearly flagged as a worked example, not prescriptive

## Verification

- [ ] `markdownlint` passes locally
- [ ] `lychee` link-check passes locally
- [ ] If I changed README content, CHANGELOG.md is updated under `[Unreleased]`
- [ ] If I changed a scaffold file, the corresponding example or migration entry is updated where applicable
- [ ] If I changed loading instructions, I verified them against the latest Claude Code docs

## Anything reviewers should know

<!-- Tradeoffs, alternative approaches you ruled out, follow-up work this enables. -->
